reasoning: The question asks whether Alexandra Kosteniuk's rating on the Wikipedia FIDE rankings page will be at least 1% higher than on August 17, 2025 (which would be approximately 2499+). Since FIDE officially updates ratings monthly, reaching a 25-point increase from her 2474 rating requires an exceptionally strong performance in classical chess over one or multiple events (gaining ~25 Elo at a 2470+ level is highly demanding for a veteran player). Kosteniuk did not reach the finals of the July 2025 FIDE Women's World Cup (won by Divya Deshmukh) and has been mostly playing speed chess in August (WSCC), making an immediate massive jump on the August or September Classical FIDE list improbable. For the closest date (Aug 24), the Wikipedia page is very unlikely to show a change from the current month's ratings unless there's an overdue update reflecting a theoretical major World Cup gain, which isn't supported by the tournament results. For further dates (Nov 2025 and Feb 2026), uncertainty naturally rises as she participates in more classical tournaments (e.g., Grand Prix, Grand Swiss), allowing more variance and opportunities to secure a 1% Elo gain, pushing the probabilities up toward 10-15%.
